# services.suricata

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.suricata`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.suricata.disabledRules` | `list of string` | List of rules that should be disabled. |
| `services.suricata.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable Suricata. |
| `services.suricata.enabledSources` | `list of string` | List of sources that should be enabled. Currently sources which require a secret-code are not supported. |
| `services.suricata.package` | `package` | The suricata package to use. |
| `services.suricata.settings` | `open submodule of (YAML 1.1 value)` | Suricata settings |
| `services.suricata.settings.af-packet` | `null or (list of (open submodule of (YAML 1.1 value)))` | Linux high speed capture support. |
| `services.suricata.settings.af-packet.*.interface` | `string` | af-packet capture interface, see [upstream docs reagrding tuning](https://docs.suricata.io/en/latest/performance/tuning-considerations.html#af-packet). |
| `services.suricata.settings.af-xdp` | `null or (list of (open submodule of (YAML 1.1 value)))` | Linux high speed af-xdp capture support, see [docs/capture-hardware/af-xdp](https://docs.suricata.io/en/suricata-7.0.3/capture-hardware/af-xdp.html). |
| `services.suricata.settings.af-xdp.*.interface` | `string` | af-xdp capture interface, see [upstream docs](https://docs.suricata.io/en/latest/capture-hardware/af-xdp.html). |
| `services.suricata.settings.app-layer` | `null or (submodule)` | app-layer configuration, see [upstream docs](https://docs.suricata.io/en/latest/rules/app-layer.html). |
| `services.suricata.settings.app-layer.error-policy` | `one of "drop-flow", "pass-flow", "bypass", "drop-packet", "pass-packet", "reject", "ignore"` | The error-policy setting applies to all app-layer parsers. Values can be "drop-flow", "pass-flow", "bypass", "drop-packet", "pass-packet", "reject" or "ignore" (the default). |
| `services.suricata.settings.app-layer.protocols` | `null or (attribute set of (open submodule of (YAML 1.1 value)))` | app-layer protocols, see [upstream docs](https://docs.suricata.io/en/latest/rules/app-layer.html). |
| `services.suricata.settings.app-layer.protocols.<name>.enabled` | `one of "yes", "no", "detection-only"` | The option "enabled" takes 3 values - "yes", "no", "detection-only". "yes" enables both detection and the parser, "no" disables both, and "detection-only" enables protocol detection only (parser disabled). |
| `services.suricata.settings.classification-file` | `string` | Suricata classification configuration file. |
| `services.suricata.settings.default-log-dir` | `string` | The default logging directory. Any log or output file will be placed here if it's not specified with a full path name. This can be overridden with the -l command line parameter. |
| `services.suricata.settings.default-rule-path` | `absolute path` | Path in which suricata-update managed rules are stored by default. |
| `services.suricata.settings.dpdk` | `null or (submodule)` | Data Plane Development Kit is a framework for fast packet processing in data plane applications running on a wide variety of CPU architectures. DPDK's Environment Abstraction Layer (EAL) provides a generic interface to low-level resources. It is a unique way how DPDK libraries access NICs. EAL creates an API for an application to access NIC resources from the userspace level. In DPDK, packets are not retrieved via interrupt handling. Instead, the application polls the NIC for newly received packets. DPDK allows the user space application to directly access memory where the NIC stores the packets. As a result, neither DPDK nor the application copies the packets for the inspection. The application directly processes packets via passed packet descriptors. See [docs/capture-hardware/dpdk](https://docs.suricata.io/en/suricata-7.0.7/capture-hardware/dpdk.html) and [docs/configuration/suricata-yaml.html#data-plane-development-kit-dpdk](https://docs.suricata.io/en/suricata-7.0.7/configuration/suricata-yaml.html#data-plane-development-kit-dpdk). |
| `services.suricata.settings.dpdk.eal-params.proc-type` | `null or string` | dpdk eal-params.proc-type, see [data plane development kit docs](https://doc.dpdk.org/guides/linux_gsg/linux_eal_parameters.html#multiprocessing-related-options). |
| `services.suricata.settings.dpdk.interfaces` | `null or (list of (open submodule of (YAML 1.1 value)))` | See upstream docs: [docs/capture-hardware/dpdk](https://docs.suricata.io/en/suricata-7.0.7/capture-hardware/dpdk.html) and [docs/configuration/suricata-yaml.html#data-plane-development-kit-dpdk](https://docs.suricata.io/en/suricata-7.0.7/configuration/suricata-yaml.html#data-plane-development-kit-dpdk). |
| `services.suricata.settings.dpdk.interfaces.*.interface` | `string` | See upstream docs: [docs/capture-hardware/dpdk](https://docs.suricata.io/en/suricata-7.0.7/capture-hardware/dpdk.html) and [docs/configuration/suricata-yaml.html#data-plane-development-kit-dpdk](https://docs.suricata.io/en/suricata-7.0.7/configuration/suricata-yaml.html#data-plane-development-kit-dpdk). |
| `services.suricata.settings.exception-policy` | `one of "auto", "drop-packet", "drop-flow", "reject", "bypass", "pass-packet", "pass-flow", "ignore"` | Define a common behavior for all exception policies. In IPS mode, the default is drop-flow. For cases when that's not possible, the engine will fall to drop-packet. To fallback to old behavior (setting each of them individually, or ignoring all), set this to ignore. All values available for exception policies can be used, and there is one extra option: auto - which means drop-flow or drop-packet (as explained above) in IPS mode, and ignore in IDS mode. Exception policy values are: drop-packet, drop-flow, reject, bypass, pass-packet, pass-flow, ignore (disable). |
| `services.suricata.settings.host-mode` | `one of "router", "sniffer-only", "auto"` | If the Suricata box is a router for the sniffed networks, set it to 'router'. If it is a pure sniffing setup, set it to 'sniffer-only'. If set to auto, the variable is internally switched to 'router' in IPS mode and 'sniffer-only' in IDS mode. This feature is currently only used by the reject\* keywords. |
| `services.suricata.settings.includes` | `null or (list of absolute path)` | Files to include in the suricata configuration. See [docs/configuration/suricata-yaml](https://docs.suricata.io/en/suricata-7.0.3/configuration/suricata-yaml.html) for available options. |
| `services.suricata.settings.logging.default-log-format` | `null or string` | The default output format. Optional parameter, should default to something reasonable if not provided. Can be overridden in an output section. You can leave this out to get the default. |
| `services.suricata.settings.logging.default-log-level` | `one of "error", "warning", "notice", "info", "perf", "config", "debug"` | The default log level: can be overridden in an output section. Note that debug level logging will only be emitted if Suricata was compiled with the --enable-debug configure option. |
| `services.suricata.settings.logging.default-output-filter` | `null or string` | A regex to filter output. Can be overridden in an output section. Defaults to empty (no filter). |
| `services.suricata.settings.logging.outputs.console.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable logging to console. |
| `services.suricata.settings.logging.outputs.file.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable logging to file. |
| `services.suricata.settings.logging.outputs.file.filename` | `string` | Filename of the logfile. |
| `services.suricata.settings.logging.outputs.file.format` | `null or string` | Logformat for logs written to the logfile. |
| `services.suricata.settings.logging.outputs.file.level` | `one of "error", "warning", "notice", "info", "perf", "config", "debug"` | Loglevel for logs written to the logfile. |
| `services.suricata.settings.logging.outputs.file.type` | `null or string` | Type of logfile. |
| `services.suricata.settings.logging.outputs.syslog.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable logging to syslog. |
| `services.suricata.settings.logging.outputs.syslog.facility` | `string` | Facility to log to. |
| `services.suricata.settings.logging.outputs.syslog.format` | `null or string` | Logformat for logs send to syslog. |
| `services.suricata.settings.logging.outputs.syslog.type` | `null or string` | Type of logs send to syslog. |
| `services.suricata.settings.logging.stacktrace-on-signal` | `null or string` | Requires libunwind to be available when Suricata is configured and built. If a signal unexpectedly terminates Suricata, displays a brief diagnostic message with the offending stacktrace if enabled. |
| `services.suricata.settings.outputs` | `null or (list of attribute set of (open submodule of (YAML 1.1 value)))` | Configure the type of alert (and other) logging you would like. Valid values for <NAME> are e. g. `fast`, `eve-log`, `syslog`, `file-store`, ... - `fast`: a line based alerts log similar to Snort's fast.log - `eve-log`: Extensible Event Format (nicknamed EVE) event log in JSON format For more details regarding the configuration, checkout the shipped suricata.yaml `shell nix-shell -p suricata yq coreutils-full --command 'yq < $(dirname $(which suricata))/../etc/suricata/suricata.yaml' ` and the [suricata documentation](https://docs.suricata.io/en/latest/output/index.html). |
| `services.suricata.settings.outputs.*.<name>.enabled` | `boolean` | Whether to enable <NAME>. |
| `services.suricata.settings.pcap` | `null or (list of (open submodule of (YAML 1.1 value)))` | Cross platform libpcap capture support. |
| `services.suricata.settings.pcap-file.checksum-checks` | `one of "yes", "no", "auto"` | Possible values are: - yes: checksum validation is forced - no: checksum validation is disabled - auto: Suricata uses a statistical approach to detect when checksum off-loading is used. (default) Warning: 'checksum-validation' must be set to yes to have checksum tested. |
| `services.suricata.settings.pcap.*.interface` | `string` | pcap capture interface, see [upstream docs](https://docs.suricata.io/en/latest/manpages/suricata.html). |
| `services.suricata.settings.plugins` | `null or (list of absolute path)` | Plugins -- Experimental -- specify the filename for each plugin shared object. |
| `services.suricata.settings.reference-config-file` | `string` | Suricata reference configuration file. |
| `services.suricata.settings.rule-files` | `list of string` | Files to load suricata-update managed rules, relative to 'default-rule-path'. |
| `services.suricata.settings.run-as.group` | `string` | Run Suricata with a specific group-id. |
| `services.suricata.settings.run-as.user` | `string` | Run Suricata with a specific user-id. |
| `services.suricata.settings.stats` | `null or (submodule)` | Engine statistics such as packet counters, memory use counters and others can be logged in several ways. A separate text log 'stats.log' and an EVE record type 'stats' are enabled by default. |
| `services.suricata.settings.stats.decoder-events` | `boolean` | Add decode events to stats |
| `services.suricata.settings.stats.decoder-events-prefix` | `string` | Decoder event prefix in stats. Has been 'decoder' before, but that leads to missing events in the eve.stats records. |
| `services.suricata.settings.stats.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable suricata global stats. |
| `services.suricata.settings.stats.interval` | `string` | The interval field (in seconds) controls the interval at which stats are updated in the log. |
| `services.suricata.settings.stats.stream-events` | `boolean` | Add stream events as stats. |
| `services.suricata.settings.threshold-file` | `string` | Suricata threshold configuration file. |
| `services.suricata.settings.unix-command` | `null or (submodule)` | Unix command socket that can be used to pass commands to Suricata. An external tool can then connect to get information from Suricata or trigger some modifications of the engine. Set enabled to yes to activate the feature. In auto mode, the feature will only be activated in live capture mode. You can use the filename variable to set the file name of the socket. |
| `services.suricata.settings.unix-command.enabled` | `boolean or value "auto" (singular enum)` | Enable unix-command socket. |
| `services.suricata.settings.unix-command.filename` | `absolute path` | Filename for unix-command socket. |
| `services.suricata.settings.vars` | `null or (submodule)` | Variables to be used within the suricata rules. |
| `services.suricata.settings.vars.address-groups` | `submodule` | The address group variables for suricata, if not defined the default value of suricata (see example) will be used. Your settings will extend the predefined values in example. |
| `services.suricata.settings.vars.address-groups.AIM_SERVERS` | `unspecified value` | AIM_SERVERS variable. |
| `services.suricata.settings.vars.address-groups.DC_SERVERS` | `unspecified value` | DC_SERVERS variable. |
| `services.suricata.settings.vars.address-groups.DNP3_CLIENT` | `unspecified value` | DNP3_CLIENT variable. |
| `services.suricata.settings.vars.address-groups.DNP3_SERVER` | `unspecified value` | DNP3_SERVER variable. |
| `services.suricata.settings.vars.address-groups.DNS_SERVERS` | `unspecified value` | DNS_SERVERS variable. |
| `services.suricata.settings.vars.address-groups.ENIP_CLIENT` | `unspecified value` | ENIP_CLIENT variable. |
| `services.suricata.settings.vars.address-groups.ENIP_SERVER` | `unspecified value` | ENIP_SERVER variable. |
| `services.suricata.settings.vars.address-groups.EXTERNAL_NET` | `unspecified value` | EXTERNAL_NET variable. |
| `services.suricata.settings.vars.address-groups.HOME_NET` | `unspecified value` | HOME_NET variable. |
| `services.suricata.settings.vars.address-groups.HTTP_SERVERS` | `unspecified value` | HTTP_SERVERS variable. |
| `services.suricata.settings.vars.address-groups.MODBUS_CLIENT` | `unspecified value` | MODBUS_CLIENT variable |
| `services.suricata.settings.vars.address-groups.MODBUS_SERVER` | `unspecified value` | MODBUS_SERVER variable. |
| `services.suricata.settings.vars.address-groups.SMTP_SERVERS` | `unspecified value` | SMTP_SERVERS variable. |
| `services.suricata.settings.vars.address-groups.SQL_SERVERS` | `unspecified value` | SQL_SERVERS variable. |
| `services.suricata.settings.vars.address-groups.TELNET_SERVERS` | `unspecified value` | TELNET_SERVERS variable. |
| `services.suricata.settings.vars.port-groups` | `null or (attribute set of string)` | The port group variables for suricata. |
